landing.kicker: A system that reduces chaos in your restaurant and increases your serving speed.
landing.headline: Universal Restaurant System
landing.subheadline: Offline-first POS for restaurants. Real-time updates, staff workflows, and a clean setup flow.

faq.sectionSubtitle: Real behaviour of the floor plan, offline mode, and guest flows — not marketing fluff.
faq.pageIntro: Answers below reflect how U.R.S. is built: SSE live updates, Dexie offline queue, role guards, and the public guest menu.

pwaSupportedBrowsers.q: Which browsers work best with U.R.S.?
pwaSupportedBrowsers.a: The product is a Progressive Web App (PWA) and works most reliably in Google Chrome and Microsoft Edge. Firefox is not supported. Safari has not been tested. Several capabilities depend on browser features that are only consistently available in Chrome and Edge.

settingsCurrencyBeforeUse.q: What must I set up before using the app for real service?
settingsCurrencyBeforeUse.a: Before you operate live, open Settings and choose the currency your restaurant will use for menus and orders. You cannot change currency while any table still has an open order—finish or close those orders first.

printerAgentDownload.q: Where do I download the Printer Agent for Windows?
printerAgentDownload.aIntro: Printer Agent runs on a Windows x64 PC at the restaurant, talks to the backend, and reports printers via heartbeat. After installing, generate an enrollment code in Manager → Settings (Printer agent section) and finish setup on that machine.
printerAgentDownload.linkUnset: Download URL is not configured for this deployment — ask your administrator or open your organisation’s Printer Agent GitHub Releases page.

printerAgentInstall.q: How do I set up the bill printer?
printerAgentInstall.aIntro: These steps assume a Windows x64 PC at the restaurant on the same network as the printer (Wi‑Fi or cable).
printerAgentInstall.step1: Download the official package (link above) and run the installer.
printerAgentInstall.step2: Windows may warn that the app is not digitally signed — you can continue the installation.
printerAgentInstall.step3: WireGuard is installed automatically. If the WireGuard app opens, activate the “urs-printer-agent” tunnel (it must show as connected / Active), then you may close the window. Without an active tunnel, the agent cannot reach Redis and bill print jobs stay stuck at “Received”.
printerAgentInstall.step4: In Manager go to Settings → Printer Agent setup, click Generate one-time code, and copy the 10-character code.
printerAgentInstall.step5: Press the Windows key, search for “URS Printer Agent Configurator”, and open the app.
printerAgentInstall.step6: Paste the enrollment code, confirm you understand the network will be scanned, select the printer IP found on the network, and click Next.
printerAgentInstall.step7: Name the main printer (for example bill-printer), then Save and close the Configurator.
printerAgentInstall.step8: Back in Settings under Bill (non-fiscal) — default printer, wait up to about 30 seconds for the printer to appear in the list, select it, and click Save.

printerAgentReconnectAfterNetwork.q: Bill printing or the Printer Agent stopped working after Wi‑Fi or a hotspot dropped. Do I need to reboot the PC?
printerAgentReconnectAfterNetwork.a: Usually not. First restart the Windows service URS Printer Agent (services.msc) — that restarts the agent process and reconnects to the backend and Redis. If the agent uses a WireGuard VPN tunnel, restart the WireGuard tunnel service for your tunnel, wait until it is active, then restart URS Printer Agent again so the tunnel is ready before the agent connects. Reboot the whole PC only if those steps still fail; it resets the entire network stack and all services.

byodStaffDevices.q: Can restaurant staff use their own phones or tablets?
byodStaffDevices.a: Yes. Teams may follow a BYOD (Bring Your Own Device) approach and use personal hardware for staff or manager workflows, alongside your organisation’s own security and acceptable-use policies.

qrSignedSecurity.q: How secure are the table QR codes?
qrSignedSecurity.a: QR payloads are digitally signed so they cannot be impersonated or forged trivially. That still does not eliminate abuse—for example ghost orders or someone photographing a code and ordering remotely (the platform also rate-limits roughly 100 calls per IP). If a QR may have been leaked or abused, regenerate the codes and replace the physical prints on tables.

bookingsSlotDuration.q: How long is a reservation, and can bookings overlap?
bookingsSlotDuration.a: By default each reservation uses a 90-minute slot. We are working on per-restaurant custom durations. The same table cannot have overlapping bookings within the same interval.

guestOrderItemList.q: Can guests see what has been ordered?
guestOrderItemList.a: Yes. On the public menu guests can open “My order” to see the list of line items for their table—names, quantities, and prices—as shown on the guest order screen.

confirmedOrderAddItems.q: After an order is confirmed, can staff still add items?
confirmedOrderAddItems.a: Yes. Even after confirming the order, staff can continue adding or updating items (online or queued offline).

waiterCallVisual.q: Why does a table turn yellow on the staff orders screen?
waiterCallVisual.a: When a guest taps “Call waiter” on the public menu, the API pushes a real-time event to your restaurant. While that call is active, the table row uses a warning (yellow) style so floor staff can spot it quickly. Snooze or resolve flows update the highlight.

tableColors.q: What do the other table colours mean?
tableColors.a: Green means the table is open without an active waiter call. Red indicates a closed or occupied table in the current model. Yellow is reserved for an active waiter request, so the three states stay easy to scan at a glance.

kitchenBarSse.q: How does the waiter know when to pick up food from the kitchen or the bar?
kitchenBarSse.a: Kitchen and Bar screens share the same live event stream as the orders view. When dishes or drinks are ready—or staff snooze a reminder—waiters see those signals so they know when to collect from the pass or bar station without refreshing the page. On supported devices, the tablet or phone that last updated that table's order also vibrates briefly so the right waiter is alerted.

kitchenBarToastDismiss.q: Why must new-order and item-deleted toasts be closed manually on Kitchen and Bar?
kitchenBarToastDismiss.a: Those toasts stay until someone dismisses them on purpose: we want to be sure staff noticed a brand-new order or that a dish should no longer be prepared. Toasts for quantity up or quantity down clear by themselves after a short time—they are informational and do not need acknowledgment.

offlineOrders.q: What happens if Wi‑Fi drops while I am taking an order?
offlineOrders.a: Staff actions can be stored locally in the browser (IndexedDB via Dexie). A background queue replays them when the connection returns, then reconciles with the server snapshot so you do not lose carts or line items mid-service.

realtimeSync.q: How do devices stay in sync?
realtimeSync.a: Each signed-in session opens a server-sent events channel scoped to your restaurant. After reconnect, a sync endpoint reapplies the authoritative table/order state, and the UI merges any pending offline work safely.

rolesAccess.a: Staff accounts focus on floor tools (orders, kitchen, bar, bookings). Managers additionally configure tables, menus, QR codes, and reports. Global administrators operate cross-tenant tools such as subscription products. JWT roles enforce these boundaries on every API call.

guestQrMenu.q: How does a guest open the menu without logging in?
guestQrMenu.a: Each table QR encodes your restaurant and table identifiers. Guests get the public menu and ordering experience in the browser, while staff remain authenticated in their own area. No guest password is required for browsing or calling the waiter from that link.

moveOrderConstraints.q: When can an order be moved to another table?
moveOrderConstraints.a: An order can be moved only when it is already confirmed, and only to a target table that is currently free (no open order).

dashboardReporting.a: KPI cards and charts aggregate closed-order revenue, staff logins, and traffic views over rolling UTC windows so you can compare the last 30 days with the previous 30. Figures respect your restaurant currency.

bookings.a: Bookings/reservations are available in the staff and manager flows so you can list, edit, or cancel holds alongside service. They use the same authenticated APIs as the rest of the product.

languages.q: Can the interface speak multiple languages?
languages.a: Yes. Choose a language from the header; translations ship with the web app bundle. Guest-facing pages and staff chrome both honour the active locale where strings exist.

orderEditsBlockedDuringPayment.q: Why can't we add or modify items while the guest is paying by card?
orderEditsBlockedDuringPayment.a: When a guest starts card checkout, the order is locked to prevent inconsistent totals. Staff can't add items or change quantities until the payment finishes or the checkout is cancelled.

subscriptionStripe.q: How do plans and billing work?
subscriptionStripe.a: Subscription tiers define limits such as tables, bars, and seats. Checkout and renewals run through Stripe; successful payment triggers the restaurant onboarding path, while managers can manage billing details from their settings when enabled.
